steps.title: Spot the cause-and-effect link
steps.content: The plant absorbs iron from soil, then the passage states iron concentrations in the soil decrease. These are presented sequentially as cause and effect—the plant's iron absorption is the reason concentrations drop.

steps.title: Match the consequence relationship
steps.content:
The first sentence describes a cause (the plant absorbs and stores iron); the blank introduces the effect (iron concentrations in the soil decrease). The relationship is cause-effect.
  • Choice A — "specifically,": signals a specific instance of a general claim; but the second clause is a consequence, not a specific case. ✗
  • Choice B — "accordingly,": signals "as a result" or "consequently" — matches cause→effect exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — "nevertheless,": signals contrast against expectation; the iron-decrease is consistent with absorption, not contrary to it. ✗
  • Choice D — "in addition,": signals adding parallel information; but iron-decrease follows from absorption, not a separate equal-weight point. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
